# 2026-03-10

## Energy
- Slept ~7h

- Morning bench time, finished polishing a cuff
- Logged a few pattern notes from the week's casework

## Open
- Order more silver wire

# 2026-02-20

## Energy

- Morning routine

## Open

# 2026-01-15

## Energy
- Slept ~7h

## Done
- Morning routine
- Cut two turquoise cabs

# 2025-12-03

## Energy
- Slept ~7h

## Done
- Morning routine
- Reflected on how a class of cases tends to unfold

## Open
- Tidy the bench, restock flux

# 2025-11-12

## Energy
- Slept ~6h

## Done
- Morning routine
- Annealed and shaped a band

## Open
- Draft notes on interview pacing

# Property case patterns

Owner: Sam Osborne

High-level reflections only — no live details.

## General observations
- Property-loss cases often hinge on routine and timing rather than any single dramatic event.
- The first plausible story is rarely the complete one; revisit assumptions once the picture settles.
- Small inconsistencies in everyday habits tend to matter more than they first appear.

## Method reminders
- Map the ordinary sequence of a day before looking for the break in it.
- Keep working notes abstract; specifics belong in the official file, not here.

# Interview methodology

Owner: Sam Osborne

General methodology notes — no names, no quotes.

## What tends to work
- Open with broad, low-pressure questions and let the timeline come out unprompted.
- Silence is a tool; people fill it, and the fill is often informative.
- Circle back to the same ground later from a different angle to test consistency.

## Lessons learned
- Memory is reconstructive; treat confident recall and accurate recall as separate things.

# Timeline reconstruction

Owner: Sam Osborne

Methodology only — kept deliberately abstract.

## Approach
- Build the timeline from fixed, verifiable anchors first, then fit the soft details around them.
- Gaps are data: note where the record goes quiet, not just where it speaks.
- Cross-check independent sources before trusting any single account of sequence.

## Pattern note
- When two timelines disagree, the discrepancy usually points at the right question to ask next.

# Cold case lessons

Owner: Sam Osborne

General lessons, no case specifics.

## Recurring lessons
- Cases go cold more often from a stalled question than from a missing fact.
- Re-reading old notes with fresh eyes surfaces assumptions that quietly hardened.
- Patience and a steady method beat intensity over the long run.

## Reminder
- Document reasoning, not just conclusions, so a later read can see where the thinking turned.

# Stone sourcing

Owner: Sam Osborne

## Thoughts
- Favor stones with stable matrix; pretty-but-fragile rarely survives setting.
- Buy turquoise in person when possible — color shifts a lot under shop light vs. daylight.
- Keep a short list of trusted parcels; ask Rita before committing to a larger lot.

## To revisit
- A small run of matched cabs for cuff work.

# Design ideas

Owner: Sam Osborne

## Concepts
- Stamped band with a single offset bezel — let the stone be the focal point.
- Cleaner shanks; less ornament, more proportion.
- A bolo with a low-profile slide for everyday wear.

## Notes
- Sketch before cutting silver; most fixes are cheaper on paper.

# Technique notes

Owner: Sam Osborne

## Bench notes
- Anneal more often than feels necessary; cracked bends cost more time than the extra heat.
- Pickle warm, not hot, to keep the finish even.
- Solder in stages — easy joints first, hardest last, stepping down flow temps.

## To try
- Tighter bezel burnishing on softer stones.

# Commission log

Owner: Sam Osborne

## In progress
- Cuff commission: turquoise selected, band shaped, bezel pending.
- A pendant concept for Martha York — waiting on a stone decision.

## Notes
- Confirm sizing in writing before final fitting.
- Photograph each finished piece for the gallery set.

# Craft fair prep

- Captured on 2026-02-27.
- Status: idea — finalize table inventory and pricing cards.

# Commission ideas

- Captured on 2026-01-22.
- Status: idea — sketch two pendant concepts before next gallery visit.

# Stone order list

- Captured on 2025-11-29.
- Status: idea — draft a small turquoise and silver wire order.

# Case 26-089 Route 66 Corridor

## Summary
Property crimes cluster on Route 66 westbound between mile 42 and 51. Three incidents in eight days. Method consistent. Working with Eddie Tsosie.

## Timeline
- 22 Sep 2026 initial dispatch mile 47 westbound.
- 24 Sep 2026 first canvass, three witnesses interviewed.
- 30 Sep 2026 lab submitted fiber sample from scene.
- 5 Oct 2026 lab returned common fiber, non-distinctive.
- 12 Oct 2026 Route 118 canvass planned.

## Persons of interest
- Two truck seen leaving mile 44 fuel station on 22 Sep footage. Registration not readable at range.
- Local sources indicate transient labour movement between Ramah and Thoreau.

## Actions open
- [ ] Subpoena fuel station cameras mile 44.
- [ ] Cross-reference tire tread with vehicle registry.
- [ ] Re-interview second Route 66 witness.
- [x] Draft Q3 closure summary for Captain Benally.

## Notes
Fibre analysis will not carry alone. Building narrative on movement and opportunity. Nothing goes into a report we cannot testify to.

# Case 26-104 Property Crime

## Summary
Rural theft off Route 118. Missing generator, unlocked shed. No forced entry to house. Fresh tire tread on the south fence line.

## Scene actions
- Cast tire tread with Eddie 08:00 Tuesday.
- Photograph tread lengths and depth against ruler.
- Interview three east-side neighbours.

## Pattern check
Second rural theft off Route 118 in six weeks. Watching for repetition.

## Custody
Nothing collected yet beyond photographs and swab from generator lock plate.

# 5 October 2026

Coffee before the sun. Rita forwarded fibre lab back overnight, common carpet. Told her I would take Route 118 with Eddie Wednesday. Requesting subpoena for fuel station cameras.

Called Mom at seven, quilt is nearly done. Kai's essay draft two arrived at supper. She moved the story about her grandmother's silver bracelet to the opening. It works.

At the bench in the evening I flushed the pickle pot and set two #8 stones on the first heavy cuff. Angela's window will be six pieces by 4 December.

# 6 October 2026

Tuesday interviews. Two of three witnesses came in on time. One rescheduled for tomorrow.

Rita booked the quarterly detective review for 22 October at 14:00. Angela emailed asking for the December window pieces, sent the piece list and she confirmed 725 for the heavy cuffs.

Copper barked at a jackrabbit twice tonight. Cleaned the workshop dust filter after supper. Bed at ten.

# 12 October 2026

Route 118 canvass with Eddie. Mile markers 12 to 20 in daylight. Two useful conversations, one property with a rural game camera that might have caught a plate. Deputy Yazzie riding along for the last stop.

Sent Kai's essay draft two back with three notes. She kept the bracelet lead-in and cut two duplicate sentences.

Booked flea market booth for 1 November. Ordered sterling round wire, twelve gauge, ten ounces.

# Anneal heavy cuff blanks - October 2026

Six blanks cut from twelve-gauge round wire. Annealed at dull cherry, quenched in water. Two shaped over the mandrel this session. Set aside for stone selection Friday.

## Stones on hand
- #8 turquoise, four cabs from Mateo's Battle Mountain parcel.
- Kingman, three cabs, one high-dome.
- Royston, two cabs matched pair.

## Next
- Bezel the Royston pair tomorrow.
- Set stones on the two heavy cuffs by weekend.

# 2026 Pricing

## Cuffs
- Heavy sterling cuff, standard turquoise: 500 to 700.
- Heavy sterling cuff, #8 or premium: 700 to 900.
- Light sterling cuff: 350 to 500.

## Bolo ties
- Sterling with common stone: 250 to 400.
- Sterling with premium stone or dual inlay: 400 to 500.

## Belt buckles
- Sterling stamped border: 600 to 800.
- Sterling with cabochon or figure: 800 to 1200.

## Notes
Angela at Turquoise Trail asked to bump heavy cuffs to 725 for the December window. Etsy pricing follows same bands.

# Mateo Reyes Stones

## Contact
Mateo Reyes, Reyes Stone Traders. Phone +52 555 123 7788. Email mateo.reyes.stones@gmail.com. Ships from Sonora.

## Notes
Reliable on #8 turquoise. Good spider web cabs in three of nine last parcel. Pay via wire, tracked FedEx. Never haggle below his opening.

## History
- Nov 2025 parcel of Kingman, took four cabs.
- Feb 2026 parcel of Royston, took three cabs.
- Oct 2026 parcel of #8, took four cabs (2, 3, 6, 8).

# Thomas Kee

## Contact
Thomas Kee, silversmith, Santa Fe. Studio near Canyon Road. Phone 505-555-0489.

## Notes
Mentor since 2018. Trained me on heavy stamping and stone setting. Runs a small die-making side operation. Ram's-head die from Kenji Aoki arrived October 2026.

## Visits
- Quarterly runs to Santa Fe for wholesale wire and sheet.
- Next visit 24 October 2026, planning bench time with the new die.

# 1978 Ford F-150 Restoration Master Log

## Vehicle
1978 Ford F-150, 300 inline-six, NP-435 four-speed manual, long bed, two-wheel drive. Bought August 2023 from a rancher near Ramah. Frame solid, cab straight.

## Progress
- Engine rebuilt 2024, runs smoothly.
- Transmission rebuilt 2025.
- Bed floor patched, needs paint.
- Wiring harness stripped, ready for new fuse block.
- Heater core new (from Ben, Farmington swap, October 2026).

## Next phase - wiring
- [ ] Order Painless harness kit.
- [ ] Install new fuse block behind dash.
- [ ] Route heater core hoses.
- [ ] Test all cluster gauges.

## Target
Driver-quality restoration by summer 2027. Not concours.

# Observing Log

## Rig
Celestron NexStar 8SE, mounted on the ranch back patio. Skyglow low from Gallup, dark to the south and east.

## October 2026
- 3 Oct - Saturn near meridian, Cassini division sharp. Ring tilt beautiful.
- 8 Oct - Copper barked at a jackrabbit right through Jupiter. Moved him inside.
- 11 Oct - New moon weekend. Andromeda, M33, and NGC 891 in one session. Cold enough for the down jacket.

## Upcoming
- 13 Nov 2026 - Uranus at opposition.
- Winter observing for M42 as the nights lengthen.

# HVAC Replacement Plan

## Existing
2003 gas furnace, marginal AC. Ductwork sound. Ranch two thousand two hundred square feet.

## Plan
Heat pump with resistance backup. Cold air infiltration around the west door will get addressed at the same time.

## Quotes needed
- Chee Heating and Air.
- Southwest Comfort.
- Gallup Mechanical.

## Budget
Ten thousand cap. HYSA reserved after Christmas expenses.

# Kai College Apps 2026

## Schools
- University of New Mexico (criminal justice track).
- New Mexico State University.
- Fort Lewis College.
- Northern Arizona University.
- Colorado Mesa University.

## Recommenders
- Ms. Nakai, English.
- Coach Begay, cross country.
- Deputy Yazzie (criminal justice track).

## Timeline
- Essay draft 3 by 5 November.
- Applications submitted by 13 November.
- Fee waiver documents to counselor.

## Dad's notes
Kai's essay is strongest when it starts with Sarah's silver bracelet. She should not spend the essay explaining her mother's passing. Let the bracelet do the work.

# Dorothy 75th Birthday Plan - 22 March 2027

## Location
Dorothy's house in Window Rock.

## Guests
Ben and family. Kai. Martha. Maybe Aunt Lois if she is well.

## Food
Cake and pan dulce from the bakery on Highway 264. Mutton stew if Dorothy wants to make it herself.

## Gifts
- Sterling cuff with blue coral inlay, hand-stamped border. Design started December 2026.
- Photo album of family in the past decade printed at the Gallup shop.

## Travel
Drive up Sunday morning. Kai comes if not away at a track meet.

# Reading Log 2026

## Currently reading
- The Big Sky, A. B. Guthrie Jr. Second time through.
- The Killer Angels, Michael Shaara. New copy from the Ramah library sale.

## Finished this year
- Blood and Thunder by Hampton Sides.
- Empire of the Summer Moon by S. C. Gwynne.
- News of the World by Paulette Jiles.
- The Sisters Brothers by Patrick deWitt.
- All the Pretty Horses by Cormac McCarthy (re-read).

## Waiting
- Book on the Long Walk (recommendation from Martha).
- A new print of Angle of Repose. Wallace Stegner is still teacher enough.

# Reading Wishlist

## Western mystery
- Any Craig Johnson I have not yet read.
- Tony Hillerman full re-read winter 2026-27.
- New Anne Hillerman.

## American West history
- Ted Morgan's Wilderness at Dawn.
- Bury My Heart at Wounded Knee (own but re-read).

# Etsy Playbook 2026-27

## Voice
Plain-spoken. Third person minimal. No exclamation marks. British spelling for prose, American for proper nouns.

## Photography
Morning north light. One wrist shot, one stone close-up, one full profile. Neutral linen background.

## Descriptions
State the piece, the stones, the weight, and the wrist or belt size. Include care notes at the end.

## Shipping
FedEx Ground domestic. Insured to piece value plus ten percent. Newsprint plus foam plus silica bag. Signature not required.

# Interview Framework

## Opening
Introduce badge. State purpose. Confirm free to leave if not in custody.

## Middle
Open question first. Follow the thread. Use silence.

## Close
Ask what I might have missed. Provide card. Note follow-up commitment.

## Notes
Write down what people did with their hands, not just what they said.

# Piece cards for Turquoise Trail December Window 2026

## Heavy sterling cuff, #8 turquoise, 7.25 inch
Hand-forged heavy sterling cuff. Set with a natural Nevada #8 turquoise cabochon. Traditional twist wire border. Hallmarked SO. Wrist 7.25 inches.

## Heavy sterling cuff, #8 turquoise, 7.5 inch
Companion piece to the seven and a quarter inch heavy cuff. Cabochon with fine spider web matrix. Hallmarked SO.

## Kingman turquoise cuff, 6.75 inch
Mid-weight sterling cuff with Kingman cabochon. Stamped border. Softer blue tone. Hallmarked SO.

## Royston cuff, 7 inch
Sterling cuff with Royston turquoise. Warm green undertones. Stamped feather motif. Hallmarked SO.

## Bolo tie, stamped tips, sterling
Sterling bolo tie tips stamped with cross and step motif. Braided leather cord thirty six inches. Slide finished in traditional pattern.

## Bolo tie, lapis and sterling
Sterling slide set with lapis cabochon. Companion tips in polished sterling. Braided leather cord thirty six inches.

# Route 118 Map Notes

## Sections
- Mile 0 to 8, high-desert scrub, few properties, easy visibility.
- Mile 8 to 12, one gas station and cluster of trailers.
- Mile 12 to 20, ranches spaced roughly a mile apart. Recent theft cluster here.
- Mile 20 to 24, cattle guard and county line.

## Contact list
- Yazzie ranch, mile 13.
- Chee family, mile 15.
- Reyes homestead, mile 18.

## Notes
Cell signal drops between mile 14 and 18. Radio only.
